title: Methods for using machine learning and mechanistic models for biological feature mapping with multiparametric mri
abstract: Described here are systems and methods for generating and implementing a hybrid machine learning and mechanistic model to produce biological feature maps, or other measurements of biological features, based on an input of multiparametric magnetic resonance or other images. The hybrid model can include a combination of a machine learning model and a mechanistic model that takes as an input multiparametric MRI, or other imaging, data to generate biological feature maps (e.g., tumor cell density maps), or other measures or predictions of biological features (e.g., tumor cell density). The hybrid models have capabilities of learning individual-specific relationships between imaging features and biological features.
claims:
Claims 1. A method for constructing and implementing a hybrid machine learning and mechanistic model to predict biological feature data, the steps of the method comprising: constructing a hybrid machine learning and mechanistic model by: (i) accessing training data with a computer system, the training data including labeled data and unlabeled data; (ii) training a machine learning model based on the training data, generating output that includes trained weights; (iii) augmenting the machine learning model using a mechanistic model that mathematically describes at least one of a biological process or a physiological process associated with a biological feature, generating a hybrid model comprising a hybrid machine learning and mechanistic model; and generating predicted biological feature data by inputting multiparametric magnetic resonance images to the hybrid model. 2. The method as recited in claim 1, wherein the mechanistic model is a proliferation-invasion model that mathematically describes a rate of change of cell density as a function of invasion of cells into nearby tissues and proliferation of cells. 3. The method as recited in claim 1, wherein the labeled data in the training data comprises image-localized biopsy data and the unlabeled data in the training data comprises multiparametric magnetic resonance images. 4. The method as recited in claim 3, wherein the labeled data are augmented with virtual biopsy data comprising cell density data that are generated using the mechanistic model. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the machine learning model is a semi- supervised learning (SSL) model. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the SSL model is a graph-based SSL model, and wherein augmenting the machine learning model using the mechanistic model comprises estimating edge weights as a function of localized features in the unlabeled data and of cell density values estimated by the mechanistic model. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the SSL model includes incorporating a Proliferation-Invasion (PI) -estimated regional cell density into a graph-based SSL using: G = argmin /Â¾ wherein L is a number of biopsy samples in a training dataset, yi is a pathologically measured tumor cell density for the l-th biopsy sample, x ( contains features computed from a localized region of multiparametric MRI corresponding to a biopsy location and a tumor density prediction of the mechanistic model, /(x ; ) is a predictive function for cell density, (y ; â /(x ; )) 2 is a loss function that measures a discrepancy between the pathologically measured cell density and a predicted density of each biopsy sample, / is a function on a reproducing kernel Hilbert space (RKHS), H K , with a Mercer kernel K, H/ll! is a norm on H K , g A is a tuning parameter, f contains predictive density for each 

 labeled and unlabeled sample, w i; is a sum of all edge weights in a graph used by the graph-based SSL. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the multiparametric magnetic resonance images include images of T1 with contrast, T2 weighted, Echo-planar imaging (EPI) with contrast, mean diffusivity (MD), fractional anisotropy (FA), and relative cerebral blood volume (rCBV). 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the hybrid model includes tuning parameters configured to minimize a mean absolute prediction error (MAPE) of the region of interest. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the tuning parameters include patient- specific tuning parameters trained for each subject to minimize the MAPE of the specific subject. 11. The method of claim 9, wherein the tuning parameters includes uniform tuning parameters configured to minimize the MAPE across all subjects. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the mechanistic model is a Proliferation Invasion (PI) model of the form: wherein c(x,t) is a tumor cell density, D(x) is a net rate of diffusion, p is a net rate of proliferation, and if is a cell carrying capacity. 13. A method for cell density mapping of a region-of-interest (ROI) of a subject with magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), the method comprising: a) selecting at least one region of an image of the subject corresponding to a location of a biopsy sample; b) generating multiparametric MR data of the subject corresponding to the at least one region of an image; c) providing the data from a) and b) to a hybrid model formed of a trained machine learning model and a mechanistic model to create a cell density map; d) displaying the cell density map for the region of interest based upon the hybrid model. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the machine learning model includes a semi-supervised learning (SSL) framework that includes labeled data comprising biopsy samples and unlabeled data comprising biopsy-free sub-regions of a tumor. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the SSL framework includes incorporating a Proliferation-Invasion (PI) -estimated regional cell density into a graph- based SSL using: L f * = argmin g Y (y ; - f(x ) 2 + g A \\f ||| + Yl f G WÎ¯ /Â¾ 7~t Ã¥i w i 

 wherein L is a number of biopsy samples in a training dataset, yi is a pathologically measured tumor cell density for the l-th biopsy sample, x ( contains features computed from a localized region of multiparametric MRI corresponding to a biopsy location and a tumor density prediction of the mechanistic model, /(x ( ) is a predictive function for cell density, (y ; â /(x ; )) 2 is a loss function that measures a discrepancy between the pathologically measured cell density and a predicted density of each biopsy sample, / is a function on a reproducing kernel Hilbert space (RKHS), H K , with a Mercer kernel K, H/ll! is a norm on H K , g A is a tuning parameter, f contains predictive density for each labeled and unlabeled sample, w i; is a sum of all edge weights in a graph used by the graph-based SSL. 16. The method of claim 13, wherein multiparametric MR data includes images of T1 with contrast, T2 weighted, Echo-planar imaging (EPI) with contrast, mean diffusivity (MD), fractional anisotropy (FA), and relative cerebral blood volume (rCBV). 17. The method of claim 13, wherein the hybrid model includes tuning parameters configured to minimize a mean absolute prediction error (MAPE) of the region of interest. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the tuning parameters include patient- specific tuning parameters trained for each subject to minimize the MAPE of the specific subject. 19. The method of claim 17, wherein the tuning parameters includes uniform tuning parameters configured to minimize the MAPE across all subjects. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the mechanistic model is a Proliferation Invasion (PI) model of the form: wherein c(x,t) is a tumor cell density, D(x) is a net rate of diffusion, p is a net rate of proliferation, and if is a cell carrying capacity. 21. The method of claim 13, wherein the biopsy sample is a virtual biopsy sample where cell density information for the selected at least one region of an image is provided by the mechanistic model. 22. The method of claim 13, wherein the biopsy sample is a real biopsy sample taken from the subject. 23. A method for generating cell density maps using a hybrid machine learning and mechanistic model of proliferation-invasion, the steps of the method comprising: (a) accessing training data comprising labeled data and unlabeled data, wherein the labeled data comprise image-localized biopsy data and the unlabeled data comprise multiparametric magnetic resonance images; (b) training a machine learning model on the training data, generating output as a trained machine learning model; 

 (c) inputting the training data to a mechanistic model of proliferation- invasion (PI), generating output as Pi-based cell density data; (d) training a hybrid machine learning and mechanistic model on the training data and the Pi-based cell density data, generating output as a trained hybrid model comprising a predictive function that relates localized magnetic resonance image features and cell density; and (e) inputting multiparametric magnetic resonance images to the trained hybrid model, generating output as one or more cell density maps that depict a spatial distribution of cell density predicted based on localized magnetic resonance image features in the multiparametric magnetic resonance images. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein training the hybrid machine learning model further comprises regularizing the trained hybrid model using the multiparametric magnetic resonance images in the training data and the Pi-based cell density data to a Laplacian matrix. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein the trained hybrid model is regularized using a Laplacian matrix that is generated by converting the multiparametric magnetic resonance images in the training data and the Pi-based cell density data. 26. A method for constructing and implementing a hybrid machine learning and mechanistic model to predict spatially and/or temporally resolved data, the steps of the method comprising: 

 constructing a hybrid machine learning and mechanistic model by: (i) accessing training data with a computer system, the training data including labeled data and unlabeled data; (ii) training a machine learning model based on the training data, generating output that includes trained weights; (iii) augmenting the machine learning model using a mechanistic model that mathematically describes behavior of a system , generating a hybrid model comprising a hybrid machine learning and mechanistic model; and generating predicted spatially and/or temporally resolved data by inputting images and/or 2D/3D data maps to the hybrid model. 27. The method as recited in claim 26, wherein the mechanistic model is a proliferation-invasion model that mathematically describes a rate of change of a density of a given population of items as a function of invasion of the item into nearby locations and increase of items. 28. The method as recited in claim 26, wherein the labeled data in the training data comprises image-localized data and the unlabeled data in the training data comprises at least one type of image or 2D/3D dataset. 29. The method as recited in claim 28, wherein the labeled data are augmented with virtual data that are generated using the mechanistic model. 30. The method of claim 26, wherein the machine learning model is a semi- supervised learning (SSL) model. 31. The method of claim 30, wherein the semi-supervised learning (SSL) model includes labeled data comprising sampled and quantified data and unlabeled data comprising sample-free and non-quantified sub-regions of a population of items. 32. The method of claim 30, wherein the SSL model is a graph-based SSL model, and wherein augmenting the machine learning model using the mechanistic model comprises estimating edge weights as a function of localized features in the unlabeled data values estimated by the mechanistic model. 33. The method of claim 30, wherein the SSL model includes incorporating a mechanistic model estimated regional density of a population of items into a graph- based SSL using: f * = argmin /e% wherein L is a number of sampled datapoints in a training dataset, yi is a pathologically measured population density for the l-th sampled datapoint, x ( contains features computed from a localized region of multiparametric images or 2 D/3 D datasets corresponding to a sampled location and a prediction of the mechanistic model, /(x ( ) is a predictive function for population density, (y ; â /(x ; )) 2 is a loss function that measures a discrepancy between the measured or quantified population density and a predicted density of each sampled datapoint, / is a function on a reproducing kernel Hilbert space (RKHS), H K , with a Mercer kernel K, \\f \\ is a norm on H K , g A is a tuning 

 parameter, f contains predictive density for each labeled and unlabeled sample, w i; is a sum of all edge weights in a graph used by the graph-based SSL. 34. The method of claim 26, further comprising a multiparametric image. 35. The method of claim 26, wherein the hybrid model includes tuning parameters configured to minimize a mean absolute prediction error (MAPE) of a region of interest. 36. The method of claim 35, wherein the tuning parameters include tuning parameters trained for each subject to minimize the MAPE of a specific subject. 37. The method of claim 35, wherein the tuning parameters includes uniform tuning parameters configured to minimize the MAPE across all subjects. 38. The method of claim 37, wherein the mechanistic model is a Proliferation Invasion (PI) model of the form: wherein c(x,t) is a density of a population of items, D(x) is a net rate of diffusion, of items, p is a net rate of proliferation of items, and K is a carrying capacity of items. 39. The method of claim 26, further comprising a sampled datapoint where population density information for a selected at least one region of an image is provided by the mechanistic model. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein the sampled datapoint is a real sampled datapoint taken from the subject.
